title: ConfigMaps, Secrets, Environment, Projection, Rotation, and Change Propagation · Kubernetes Retrieval
summary: An on-call handoff reconstructs configmaps, secrets, environment, projection, rotation, and change propagation from delayed object status, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the configuration and secret propagation contract under changed capacity. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for configuration and secret propagation contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Create and consume ConfigMaps through keys, files, env, envFrom, command arguments, immutable objects, and size or naming boundaries.
Distinguish base64 representation, API and etcd encryption, transport protection, RBAC, node exposure, application use, and external secret authority.
Compose Secret, ConfigMap, service account token, downward API, and cluster trust bundle projections with scoped paths, modes, audiences, and refresh behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Secret Rotation Boundary
steps.instruction:
review case — A review release embeds a credential in Git, mixes immutable and rotating config, uses env projection where a file watch is required, and grants broad Secret reads. Separate creation, consumption, update propagation, encryption, RBAC, and rotation evidence. Competency probe: Design short-lived credentials, audience, rotation, revocation, redaction, audit, reload, failure, and recovery evidence across workload and external systems.

Inspect the K8s Secret Rotation Boundary trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Secret Rotation Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design short-lived credentials, audience, rotation, revocation, redaction, audit, reload, failure, and recovery evidence across workload and external systems. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Secret Rotation Boundary, keep K8s Secret Rotation Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For K8s Secret Rotation Boundary, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Secret Rotation Boundary, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Secret Rotation Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Separate creation, consumption, update propagation, encryption, RBAC, and rotation evidence. Competency probe: Design short-lived credentials, audience, rotation, revocation, redaction, audit, reload, failure, and recovery evidence across workload and external systems.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Secret Rotation Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Replacing a Secret object proves every consumer stopped using the old credential.
